input: Sentence: If the file system supports directories, then generally file name uniqueness is enforced within the context of each directory Entity1: File System (TOPIC). Entity2: Directory (CONCEPT).
Sentence: This may be implemented by associating the file name with an index in a table of contents or an inode in a Unix-like file system Entity1: File System (TOPIC). Entity2: Inode (CONCEPT).
Sentence: may also include capabilities to create additional links to a directory (hard links in Unix), to rename parent links (" Entity1: Directory (CONCEPT). Entity2: UNIX (OTHER).
Sentence: This allows for efficient random access and eliminates external fragmentation Entity1: Fragmentation (CONCEPT). Entity2: External Fragmentation (CONCEPT).
Sentence: A file system manages files and directories, providing a way for the operating system to read and write data Entity1: File System (TOPIC). Entity2: Operating System (OTHER).
Sentence: A mount point is used for attaching one file system to another Entity1: Directory (CONCEPT). Entity2: File System (TOPIC).

Sentence: The operating system provides system calls or commands to perform these operations while ensuring data security and proper resource management Entity1: Security (TOPIC). Entity2: System Calls (TOPIC).
Sentence: A preemptive scheduling algorithm picks a process and lets it run for a maximum of some fixed time Entity1: Process (CONCEPT). Entity2: Preemptive Scheduling (METHOD).

Sentence: Doing preemptive scheduling requires having a clock interrupt occur at the end of the time interval to give control of the CPU back to the scheduler Entity1: Interrupt (CONCEPT). Entity2: Preemptive Scheduling (METHOD).
Sentence: The multilevel feedback queue scheduling algorithm allows a process to move between queues Entity1: Process (CONCEPT). Entity2: Multilevel Feedback Queue (ALGORITHM).
Sentence: Waiting Time: A process has to wait for any resources if there is a high demand for the resource Entity1: Process (CONCEPT). Entity2: wait (METHOD).
Sentence: Waiting Time: A process has to wait for any resources if there is a high demand for the resource Entity1: Waiting Time (CONCEPT). Entity2: wait (METHOD).
